items.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
items.authors: Zifan Carl Guo, Laura Ruis, Jacob Andreas et al.
items.abstract: When does training language models (LMs) to generate explanations of their predictions yield faithful introspection, rather than superficial imitation? We study LMs trained to explain which features of their inputs influenced their behavior, using models' counterfactual behavior on modified inputs a...

items.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
items.authors: Sergio Hernández-Gutiérrez, Matteo Merler, Ilze Amanda Auzina et al.
items.abstract: LLM agents increasingly act over long horizons, where a single trajectory can contain hundreds or thousands of actions. In these settings, outcome-only rewards provide too sparse guidance, failing to inform the model about the goodness of intermediate actions. Dense supervision methods aim to solve ...

items.title: FaceMoE: Mixture of Experts for Low-Resolution Face Recognition
items.abstract: Low-resolution face recognition (LR-FR) remains a challenging task due to poor feature extraction and aggregation, as probe images often contain limited identity information resulting from extreme degradations such as blur, occlusion, and low contrast. Additionally, the domain gap between high-resol...

items.title: GEAR: Guided End-to-End AutoRegression for Image Synthesis
items.authors: Bin Lin, Zheyuan Liu, Chenguo Lin et al.
items.abstract: Visual generative models are typically trained in two stages. A tokenizer is first trained for reconstruction and then frozen, after which a generator is trained on its discrete indices or continuous latents. This decoupling leaves the tokenizer unaware of what the generator finds easy to model. We ...

items.title: DVG-WM: Disentangled Video Generation Enables Efficient Embodied World Model for Robotic Manipulation
items.authors: Ziyu Shan, Zhenyu Wu, Xiaofeng Wang et al.
items.abstract: Video-based embodied world models provide an appealing substrate for robotic manipulation by predicting future states, yet current approaches remain limited by a fundamental entanglement: accurately modeling dynamics typically requires low-level temporal reasoning, while producing high-resolution fr...

items.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
items.authors: Marcel Torne, Anubha Mahajan, Abhijnya Bhat et al.
items.abstract: Reward design remains a central bottleneck for autonomous robot policy improvement, especially in long-horizon manipulation tasks where sparse success labels provide too little signal and binary preferences collapse many competing notions of quality into one ambiguous signal. We introduce Freeform P...
